name: Turnover Cost Calculator
description: Calculates total cost of employee turnover based on direct and indirect costs, using industrial engineering standards (ISO 30400, Lean, Six Sigma, WERC).

inputs.label: Number of Employees
inputs.businessContext: Total headcount at the start of the period.

inputs.label: Annual Turnover Rate
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of employees leaving per year (voluntary + involuntary).

inputs.label: Average Annual Salary per Employee
inputs.businessContext: Average total compensation (base + benefits) per employee.

inputs.label: Onboarding Cost per Hire
inputs.businessContext: Training materials, orientation, IT setup, mentor time.

inputs.label: Lost Productivity Duration
inputs.businessContext: Average months of reduced output per new hire (ramp-up period).

inputs.label: Productivity Factor during Ramp-up
inputs.businessContext: Average productivity level during ramp-up (e.g., 50% means half output).

inputs.label: Overtime Cover Cost per Leaver
inputs.businessContext: Extra pay for remaining staff covering duties.

inputs.label: Quality Defect Cost per Leaver
inputs.businessContext: Rework, scrap, customer complaints due to inexperience.

inputs.label: Knowledge Loss Cost per Leaver
inputs.businessContext: Loss of institutional knowledge, intellectual property.

inputs.label: Include Indirect Costs
inputs.businessContext: Toggle to include quality, customer, and knowledge loss costs.

rules.message: Annual turnover rate must be between 0% and 100%.
Average salary must be greater than zero.
Productivity factor must be between 0% and 100%.
Lost productivity duration must be between 0 and 12 months.

rules.condition: include_indirect_costs == true OR (quality_defect_cost_per_leaver == 0 AND customer_impact_cost_per_leaver == 0 AND knowledge_loss_cost_per_leaver == 0)
rules.message: If indirect costs are excluded, set quality, customer, and knowledge loss costs to zero.

thresholds.message: Turnover rate exceeds 20% – consider retention initiatives.
Total turnover cost exceeds 20% of total payroll – urgent action recommended.
Productivity loss accounts for more than 30% of total turnover cost – review onboarding and ramp-up processes.

formulas.description: Number of employees leaving per year
Sum of direct costs per leaver multiplied by number of leavers
formulas.expression: num_leavers * (recruitment_cost_per_hire + onboarding_cost_per_hire + training_cost_per_hire + severance_cost_per_leaver + exit_interview_cost_per_leaver + overtime_cover_cost_per_leaver)

formulas.description: Cost of lost productivity during ramp-up period
Indirect costs (quality, customer, knowledge) if included
Primary result – total cost of turnover
Turnover cost as percentage of total payroll

primary.label: Total Turnover Cost
primary.description: Annual total cost of employee turnover

direct_costs.label: Direct Costs
direct_costs.description: Recruitment, onboarding, training, severance, exit interviews, overtime cover

lost_productivity_cost.label: Lost Productivity Cost
lost_productivity_cost.description: Cost of reduced output during ramp-up

indirect_costs.label: Indirect Costs
indirect_costs.description: Quality defects, customer impact, knowledge loss

turnover_cost_percentage_of_payroll.label: Turnover Cost as % of Payroll
turnover_cost_percentage_of_payroll.description: Percentage of total payroll consumed by turnover

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Recruitment Efficiency Gap
hiddenLossDrivers.description: If recruitment cost per hire > 10% of avg salary, consider optimizing sourcing channels.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Extended Onboarding Duration
hiddenLossDrivers.description: If lost productivity months > 3, onboarding process may be inefficient.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Low Training Effectiveness
hiddenLossDrivers.description: If productivity factor < 60%, training programs may need improvement.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Knowledge Retention Risk
hiddenLossDrivers.description: If knowledge loss cost per leaver > 10% of avg salary, implement knowledge management systems.

suggestedActions.label: Implement Retention Program
suggestedActions.description: Target high-turnover departments with engagement surveys and career development plans.

suggestedActions.label: Improve Onboarding Process
suggestedActions.description: Reduce ramp-up time by structured mentoring and standardized training.

suggestedActions.label: Optimize Recruitment Channels
suggestedActions.description: Use data analytics to identify most cost-effective sourcing methods.

suggestedActions.label: Deploy Knowledge Management System
suggestedActions.description: Capture critical knowledge from departing employees via exit interviews and documentation.

suggestedActions.label: Strengthen Quality Training
suggestedActions.description: Reduce defect costs by Six Sigma or Lean training for new hires.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Adjusted Cost
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Total turnover cost adjusted for data confidence (e.g., ±20% uncertainty)
